notes: Censorship-resistance and abuse-control hardening: (1) Disguise — move the panel, login and subscription to secret paths you choose (Settings → Network → Disguise), so probing the default /admin or /login just returns a generic page; the shipped worker also encodes protocol strings (vless/trojan) out of the build. (2) Per-ISP clean-IP pool — point Smart IP mode at a folder of per-carrier files (mci.txt, mtn.txt, …) and each user is auto-served IPs for their operator. (3) Request-count quota backstop — per-user request/daily-request limits that enforce even when byte accounting is thin (backend-forward mode). All opt-in; existing installs unchanged.
